prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation on benzene using DFT at B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, in PySCF (RKS). The geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied benzene.xyz (single-point; not re-optimized at this level). SCF converged in 8 cycles. No warnings were reported. The molecule has 12 atoms (6C + 6H), 21 occupied orbitals out of 222 total.

Key results:
- HOMO energy: −7.0468329 eV (orbital index 20)
- LUMO energy: −0.41574728 eV (orbital index 21)
- HOMO–LUMO gap (headline value): 6.6310857 eV

The HOMO and HOMO−1 are nearly degenerate (Δ ≈ 0.0005 eV), consistent with benzene's e₁g π-MO symmetry. LUMO and LUMO+1 are likewise nearly degenerate (Δ ≈ 0.0006 eV), reflecting the e₂u π* orbitals. The HOMO–LUMO gap is a Koopmans-style orbital energy difference, not an excitation energy.

Integrity: trustworthy = true (energy finite, SCF converged, schema checks all passed).
Warnings: none reported.
